summary: The full cross-area flow worked end-to-end: generated users were created, manual and transcript-derived users coexisted, publish upserted axes into the library, and a study draft was created from the published config.

assertions.details: A draft config with axes generated a 27-user cohort, was reviewed, published, and then used to create a study draft.
evidence: Runtime config `ut-runtime-87ed9824 config` generated `27/27 synthetic users` while still draft.
After publish, `/studies/new` listed `ut-runtime-87ed9824 config (published)` and saving the form produced the `ut-cross study` draft detail page.

assertions.details: Manual and generated users coexisted in the same config with distinct source badges.
evidence: After adding `ut-cross manual synthetic user`, the runtime config text showed both generated rows (`Source: generated`) and the manual row (`Source: manual`).

assertions.details: Transcript-derived and generated users coexisted in the same config.
evidence: Uploading `transcript-cross.txt`, attaching it to the runtime config, and applying guided extraction produced `Reassurance-and-Explanation Seeker`.
The filtered grid showed the transcript-derived row with `Transcript-derived` source while generated rows remained in the same config.

assertions.details: Publishing the generated-user runtime config upserted its shared axes into the Axis Library.
evidence: After publishing the runtime config, the Axis Library showed 3 saved definitions including the previously unpublished `time_pressure` axis.
The `time_pressure` row showed `Usage Count 1` and `Creation Source Persona configuration publish`.
